codeSystems.name: DocumentClassValueSet
codeSystems.title: Document Class Value Set
codeSystems.description: This is the code specifying the high-level kind of document (e.g. Prescription, Discharge Summary, Report, etc.). Note: Class code for documents comes from LOINC, and is based upon one of the following:The type of service described by the document. It is described at a very high level in Section 7.3 of the LOINC Manual. The type study performed. It was determined by identifying modalities for study reports. The section of the chart where the document is placed. It was determined from the SETs created for Claims Attachment requests.

codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the vaccine administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.title: Immunization Origin Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the data when the report of the immunization event is not based on information from the person, entity or organization who administered the vaccine. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.title: Immunization Program Eligibility
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the patient's eligibility for a vaccination program. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationSubpotentReason

codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose is considered to be subpotent. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ObservationInterpretationCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Interpretation Codes
codeSystems.description:
A categorical assessment, providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation value,    such as “normal”/ “abnormal”,”low” / “high”, “better” / “worse”, “susceptible” / “resistant”, “expected”/ “not expected”.    The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.   
   
   Notes:
   
   This is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) in R1 (Representative Realm) and in FHIR in    Observation.interpretation. Historically these values come from the laboratory domain, and these codes are extensively    used. The value set binding is extensible, so codes outside the value set that are needed for interpretation concepts    (i.e. particular meanings) that are not included in the value set can be used, and these new codes may also be added to    the value set and published in a future version.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-au-norelevantfinding: AU Assertion of No Relevant Finding

codeSystems.name: ProcedureNotPerformedReason(SNOMED-CT)
codeSystems.title: Procedure Not Performed Reason (SNOMED-CT)
codeSystems.description: Situation codes describing the reason that a procedure, which might otherwise be expected, was not performed, or a procedure that was started and was not completed. Consists of SNOMED CT codes, children of procedure contraindicated (183932001), procedure discontinued (416406003), procedure not done (416237000), procedure not indicated (428119001), procedure not offered (416064006), procedure not wanted (416432009), procedure refused (183944003), and procedure stopped (394908001).

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSIdentifierTypeAUExtended
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-identifierType - AU Extended
codeSystems.description: Extended value set of identifier types for use in an Australian context. Includes HL7 international codes plus Australian additions to create a complete set. NOTE: codes contained LACN Laboratory Accession ID and ACN Accession ID are exclusive concepts and not consider related in this terminology.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSDegreeLicenseCertificateAUExtended
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-degreeLicenseCertificate - AU Extended
codeSystems.description: hl7VS-degreeLicenseCertificate extended for use in an Australian context. Includes HL7 international codes plus Australian additions to create a complete set.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSProviderRoleAUExtended
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-providerRole - AU Extended
codeSystems.description: hl7VS-providerRole extended for use in an Australian context. Includes HL7 international codes plus Australian additions to create a complete set.

codeSystems.name: ActEncounterCodeAUExtended
codeSystems.title: ActEncounterCode - AU Extended
codeSystems.description: v3 Act Encounter Code extended for use in an Australian context. Includes HL7 international codes plus Australian additions to create a complete set.

codeSystems.name: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleTypeAUExtended
codeSystems.title: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType - AU Extended
codeSystems.description: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType extended for use in an Australian context. Includes HL7 international codes plus Australian additions to create a complete set.

codeSystems.name: ContactEntityType
codeSystems.title: Contact entity type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the purpose for which you would contact a contact party.

codeSystems.name: JurisdictionValueSet
codeSystems.title: Jurisdiction ValueSet
codeSystems.description:
This value set defines a base set of codes for country, country subdivision and region    for indicating where a resource is intended to be used.   
   
   Note: The codes for countries and country subdivisions are taken from    [ISO 3166](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html)    while the codes for "supra-national" regions are from    [UN Standard country or area codes for statistical use (M49)](http://unstats.un.org/unsd/methods/m49/m49.htm).

codeSystems.name: LocationType
codeSystems.title: Location type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the physical form of the Location.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSReAdmissionIndicator
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-re-admissionIndicator
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes which are used to specify that a patient is being re-admitted to a healthcare facility from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSBedStatus
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-bedStatus
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify the state of a bed in an inpatient setting, and is used to determine if a patient may be assigned to it or not.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSDegreeLicenseCertificate
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-degreeLicenseCertificate
codeSystems.description: Concepts specifying an educational degree (e.g., MD).  Used in the CNN datatype (names and identifiers of clinicians) in Version 2 messaging.  Used in Version 2 messaging; note that in releases of HL7 prior to 2.3.1, was also used in person names (XPN), but this use was deprecated, then withdrawn in 2.7.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAdditivePreservative
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-additivePreservative
codeSystems.description: Concepts specifying any additive introduced to the specimen before or at the time of collection.  These additives may be introduced in order to preserve, maintain or enhance the particular nature or component of the specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSSpecimenCondition
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-specimenCondition
codeSystems.description: Concepts of modes or states of being that describe the nature of a specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSRelevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-relevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify additional clinical information about the patient or specimen to report the supporting and/or suspected diagnosis and clinical findings on requests for interpreted diagnostic studies.

codeSystems.name: ActCode
codeSystems.title: v3 Code System ActCode
codeSystems.description:
A code specifying the particular kind of Act that the Act-instance represents within its class.  Constraints:
The kind of Act (e.g. physical examination, serum potassium, inpatient encounter, charge financial transaction, etc.) is specified with a code from one of several, typically external, coding systems.  The coding system will depend on the class of Act, such as LOINC for observations, etc. Conceptually, the Act.code must be a specialization of the Act.classCode. This is why the structure of ActClass domain should be reflected in the superstructure of the ActCode domain and then individual codes or externally referenced vocabularies subordinated under these domains that reflect the ActClass structure. Act.classCode and Act.code are not modifiers of each other but the Act.code concept should really imply the Act.classCode concept. For a negative example, it is not appropriate to use an Act.code "potassium" together with and Act.classCode for "laboratory observation" to somehow mean "potassium laboratory observation" and then use the same Act.code for "potassium" together with Act.classCode for "medication" to mean "substitution of potassium". This mutually modifying use of Act.code and Act.classCode is not permitted.

codeSystems.name: ActCoverageTypeCode
codeSystems.title: ActCoverageTypeCode
codeSystems.description: **Definition:** Set of codes indicating the type of insurance policy or program that pays for the cost of benefits provided to covered parties.
codeSystems.ValueSet-au-coverage-type-extended.html: Coverage Type and Self-Pay Codes - AU Extended

codeSystems.name: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.title: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.description: Domain provides codes that qualify the ActEncounterClass (ENC)

codeSystems.name: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.title: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.description: Identifies types of dispensing events

codeSystems.name: ActPriority
codeSystems.title: ActPriority
codeSystems.description:
A code or set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency,) specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.

*Discussion:* This attribute is used in orders to indicate the ordered priority, and in event documentation it indicates the actual priority used to perform the act. In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.

codeSystems.name: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.title: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.description: ***No description***

codeSystems.name: v3.ConfidentialityClassification
codeSystems.title: V3 Value SetConfidentialityClassification
codeSystems.description: Set of codes used to value Act.Confidentiality and Role.Confidentiality attribute in accordance with the definition for concept domain "Confidentiality".

codeSystems.name: GTIN
codeSystems.title: GTIN
codeSystems.description: **Description:**Global Trade Item Number is an identifier for trade items developed by GS1 (comprising the former EAN International and Uniform Code Council).

codeSystems.name: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.title: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.description: A role of a place that further classifies the setting (e.g., accident site, road side, work site, community location) in which services are delivered.

codeSystems.name: AdverseReactionAgent
codeSystems.title: Adverse Reaction Agent
codeSystems.description: The Adverse Reaction Agent value set includes values that support the recording of agents that may be responsible for causing adverse reactions. It is a broad set encompassing medicinal and non-medicinal agents.

codeSystems.name: AdverseReactionClinicalManifestation
codeSystems.title: Adverse Reaction Clinical Manifestation
codeSystems.description: The Adverse Reaction Clinical Manifestation value set supports the recording of clinical manifestations of adverse reactions (including allergies and intolerances).

codeSystems.name: AustralianMedicinesTerminologyVaccine
codeSystems.title: Australian Medicines Terminology Vaccine
codeSystems.description: The Australian Medicines Terminology (AMT) Vaccine value set includes AMT product concepts that may be used to record a vaccine product.

codeSystems.name: AssertionOfAbsence
codeSystems.title: Assertion Of Absence
codeSystems.description: The Assertion Of Absence value set includes values to represent clinical statements that broadly assert a procedure has not been done or the absence of a finding.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-au-norelevantfinding: AU Assertion of No Relevant Finding

codeSystems.name: AustralianImmunisationRegisterVaccine
codeSystems.title: Australian Immunisation Register Vaccine
codeSystems.description: The Australian Immunisation Register Vaccine value set includes values that may be used to represent a vaccine product according to the Australian Immunisation Register vaccine code formats.

codeSystems.name: AustralianIndigenousStatus
codeSystems.title: Australian Indigenous Status
codeSystems.description: The Australian Indigenous Status value set includes values to support the representation of a person's Indigenous status. That is, whether a person identifies as being of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander origin. The values are those concepts defined in the Australian Indigenous Status code system (https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/CodeSystem/australian-indigenous-status-1).

codeSystems.name: AustralianMedication
codeSystems.title: Australian Medication
codeSystems.description: The Australian Medication value set includes all Australian Medicines Terminology (AMT) product concepts that may be used for the identification of a medicine, vaccine or other therapeutic good. This is suitable for the recording of a product which may be ordered for, administered to, or used by an individual.

codeSystems.name: AustralianMedicationUse
codeSystems.title: Australian Medication Use
codeSystems.description: The Australian Medication Use value set extends the NCTS Australian Medication value set by including all Australian Medicines Terminology (AMT) product concepts that may be used for the identification of a medicine, vaccine or other therapeutic good, together with the concept 787481004 |No known medications| (SNOMED CT). This value set is suitable for recording the medication or asserting the absence of known medications in a Medication Statement.

codeSystems.name: AustralianStatesAndTerritories
codeSystems.title: Australian States and Territories
codeSystems.description: The Australian States and Territories value set includes values that represent the Australian states and territories.

codeSystems.name: AustralianVeteranStatus
codeSystems.title: Australian Veteran Status
codeSystems.description: The Australian Veteran Status value set includes values to support the representation of a person's veteran status in the Australian Defence Force. The values are those concepts defined in the Australian Veteran Status code system (https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/CodeSystem/australian-veteran-status-1).

codeSystems.name: BodySite
codeSystems.title: Body Site
codeSystems.description: The Body Site value set includes values that cover a broad range of anatomical and acquired body structures suitable for identifying a body site.

codeSystems.name: BodySiteRelativeSiteQualifier
codeSystems.title: Body Site Relative Site Qualifier
codeSystems.description: The Body Site Relative Site Qualifier value set includes values that may be used to represent a relative body site when qualifying an anatomical location.

codeSystems.name: ClinicalCondition
codeSystems.title: Clinical Condition
codeSystems.description: The Clinical Condition value set includes values that cover a broad range of clinical concepts to support the representation of conditions, including problems, diagnoses and disorders.

codeSystems.name: ClinicalFinding
codeSystems.title: Clinical Finding
codeSystems.description: The Clinical Finding value set includes a broad range of values that support the recording of disorders and clinical findings, including observations, judgments or assessments about subjects of care.

codeSystems.name: ClinicalSpecialty
codeSystems.title: Clinical Specialty
codeSystems.description: The Clinical Specialty value set includes values that may be used to describe a clinical specialty performed by an entity.

codeSystems.name: CommonLanguagesInAustralia
codeSystems.title: Common Languages in Australia
codeSystems.description: The Common Languages in Australia value set includes languages that are commonly encountered in Australia, including a comprehensive set of Australian Indigenous languages.

codeSystems.name: ConcurrentSupplyGrounds
codeSystems.title: Concurrent Supply Grounds
codeSystems.description: The Concurrent Supply Grounds value set includes values that may be used for the grounds that authorise a subsidy for the concurrent supply of an item specified in a prescription and all of its repeats.

codeSystems.name: ContactRelationshipType
codeSystems.title: Contact Relationship Type
codeSystems.description: The Contact Relationship Type value set includes values that may be used to describe the nature of the relationship between the subject and the contact person.

codeSystems.name: DateAccuracyIndicator
codeSystems.title: Date Accuracy Indicator
codeSystems.description: The Date Accuracy Indicator value set includes values that indicate the accuracy of components of a reported date.

codeSystems.name: EncounterType
codeSystems.title: Encounter Type
codeSystems.description: The Encounter Type value set includes values that can be used to represent types of healthcare interactions.

codeSystems.name: EvaluationProcedure
codeSystems.title: Evaluation Procedure
codeSystems.description: The Evaluation Procedure value set includes values that cover a broad range of procedures including specialist investigations, imaging procedures, pathology procedures, and other diagnostic investigations.

codeSystems.name: HealthcareOrganisationRoleType
codeSystems.title: Healthcare Organisation Role Type
codeSystems.description: The Healthcare Organisation Role Type value set includes values that can represent the role, use or function of a healthcare related organisation or facility.

codeSystems.name: IndividualHealthcareIdentifierRecordStatus
codeSystems.title: Individual Healthcare Identifier Record Status
codeSystems.description: The Individual Healthcare Identifier Record Status value set includes values that may be used to describe the classification status of a record of an Individual Healthcare Identifier.

codeSystems.name: IndividualHealthcareIdentifierStatus
codeSystems.title: Individual Healthcare Identifier Status
codeSystems.description: The Individual Healthcare Identifier Status value set includes values that may be used to describe the classification status of an Individual Healthcare Identifier.

codeSystems.name: ImagingAnatomicRegionOfInterest
codeSystems.title: Imaging Anatomic Region Of Interest
codeSystems.description: The Imaging Anatomic Region of Interest value set includes values that may be used to represent the anatomic area of interest of a diagnostic imaging procedure.

codeSystems.name: ImagingProcedure
codeSystems.title: Imaging Procedure
codeSystems.description: The Imaging Procedure value set includes values that support the representation of medical imaging procedures.

codeSystems.name: ImmunisationAnatomicalSite
codeSystems.title: Immunisation Anatomical Site
codeSystems.description: The Immunisation Anatomical Site value set includes values that can be used to describe the anatomical site where an immunisation may be administered.

codeSystems.name: ImmunisationRouteOfAdministration
codeSystems.title: Immunisation Route of Administration
codeSystems.description: The Immunisation Route of Administration value set includes values that can be used to describe the route by which a vaccine is administered.

codeSystems.name: IndicatorOfHypersensitivityOrIntoleranceToSubstance
codeSystems.title: Indicator of Hypersensitivity or Intolerance to Substance
codeSystems.description: The Indicator of Hypersensitivity or Intolerance to Substance value set includes values that may be used to indicate or state a propensity to adverse reaction to a substance. The values represent a range of concept types such as substances, medicines, clinical findings and negated allergy findings.

codeSystems.name: MedicationForm
codeSystems.title: Medication Form
codeSystems.description: The Medication Form value set includes values that represent the dose form of a medication.

codeSystems.name: MedicationIngredient
codeSystems.title: Medication Ingredient
codeSystems.description: The Medication Ingredient value set includes values that may be used to identify a substance or a medicinal product that is an ingredient of a medication.

codeSystems.name: MedicationReasonTaken
codeSystems.title: Medication Reason Taken
codeSystems.description: The Medication Reason Taken value set includes values that identify a reason why a medication has been or is being taken.

codeSystems.name: MedicineSubstitutionReason
codeSystems.title: Medicine Substitution Reason
codeSystems.description: The Medicine Substitution Reason value set includes values that may be used to record the reason for medicine substitution.

codeSystems.name: PathologyDiagnosticServiceCategory
codeSystems.title: Pathology Diagnostic Service Category
codeSystems.description: The Pathology Diagnostic Service Category value set includes values that may be used to indicate the diagnostic service where a pathology observation was performed.

codeSystems.name: PractitionerRole
codeSystems.title: Practitioner Role
codeSystems.description: The Practitioner Role value set includes values that describe the role that a practitioner is authorised to perform within an organisation.

codeSystems.name: Procedure
codeSystems.title: Procedure
codeSystems.description: The Procedure value set includes values that cover a broad range of procedures and clinical interventions that can be associated with a subject of care.

codeSystems.name: ReasonForEncounter
codeSystems.title: Reason For Encounter
codeSystems.description: The Reason For Encounter value set includes values that can be used to represent the reason an encounter takes place.

codeSystems.name: ReasonForRequest
codeSystems.title: Reason for Request
codeSystems.description: The Reason for Request value set includes values that can be used to indicate the rationale for a request. For example, this may include the reason for requesting a service or diagnostic procedure.

codeSystems.name: ReasonVaccineAdministered
codeSystems.title: Reason Vaccine Administered
codeSystems.description: The Reason Vaccine Administered value set includes values that describe why a vaccine may be administered.

codeSystems.name: ReasonVaccineNotAdministered
codeSystems.title: Reason Vaccine Not Administered
codeSystems.description: The Reason Vaccine Not Administered value set includes values that describe why a vaccine was not administered.

codeSystems.name: RelatedPersonRelationshipType
codeSystems.title: Related Person Relationship Type
codeSystems.description: The Related Person Relationship Type value set includes values that may be used to describe the nature of the relationship between the subject and the related person.

codeSystems.name: RouteOfAdministration
codeSystems.title: Route of Administration
codeSystems.description: The Route of Administration value set includes values that describe the route by which a medicine may be administered.

codeSystems.name: SeparationMode
codeSystems.title: Separation Mode
codeSystems.description: The Separation Mode value set includes values that can be used to indicate the category of discharge of a patient. Separation refers to the process by which an episode of care for an admitted patient ceases.

codeSystems.name: ServiceType
codeSystems.title: Service Type
codeSystems.description: The Service Type value set includes values that describe a broad range of service types.

codeSystems.name: SpecimenCollectionProcedure
codeSystems.title: Specimen Collection Procedure
codeSystems.description: The Specimen Collection Procedure value set includes values to represent the method by which a specimen was collected.

codeSystems.name: SpecimenType
codeSystems.title: Specimen Type
codeSystems.description: The Specimen Type value set includes values to represent a specimen collected or tested in a pathological investigation.

codeSystems.name: RCPASPIAPathologyReporting
codeSystems.title: RCPA SPIA Pathology Reporting
codeSystems.description: The RCPA SPIA Pathology Reporting value set includes standard codes for use in reporting chemical pathology, haematology, immunopathology and microbiology serology results in Australia. The values represent the observables against which results are reported. It combines several Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) Standards for Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) value sets. The composition of this value set, in referencing other value sets, does not transiently include the designations and displays defined in the referenced value sets.

codeSystems.name: Substance
codeSystems.title: Substance
codeSystems.description: The Substance value set includes values that support the representation of a broad range of substances. Included are the broadest range of substance values typically available in SNOMED CT along with the medicinal substances from the Australian Medicines Terminology.

codeSystems.name: VaccinationTargetDisease
codeSystems.title: Vaccination Target Disease
codeSystems.description: The Vaccination Target Disease value set includes values that can represent vaccine preventable diseases. The content coverage includes diseases caused by a microorganism or bacterial toxin.

codeSystems.name: spia-chemical-pathology-refset
codeSystems.title: RCPA - SPIA Chemical Pathology Value Set
codeSystems.description: Standard codes for use in reporting chemical pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

codeSystems.name: spia-haematology-refset
codeSystems.title: RCPA - SPIA Haematology Value Set
codeSystems.description: Standard codes for use in reporting haematology pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

codeSystems.name: spia-immunopathology-refset
codeSystems.title: RCPA - SPIA Immunopathology Value Set
codeSystems.description: Standard codes for use in reporting immunopathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

codeSystems.name: spia-microbiology-serology-molecular-refset
codeSystems.title: RCPA - SPIA Microbiology Serology Molecular Pathology Value Set
codeSystems.description: Standard codes for use in reporting microbiology pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference
